int queue %1. No user action is required." />
        <string id="event_MSG_QUEUE_DATA_FAILED" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) restored queue %1, but failed to restore printer driver settings. Error %2. This can occur if the driver on the destination server is newer than the driver in the migration file. Open the printer Properties dialog box on the destination computer and manually specify the appropriate printer settings." />
        <string id="event_MSG_QUEUE_PUBLISH" value="While attempting to publish the printer to the Active Directory directory service, Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) may have failed to adjust publishing settings for %1. This can occur if Printbrm.exe cannot access Active Directory. Manually publish the printer using the printer Properties dialog box." />
        <string id="event_MSG_QUEUE_UPDATING" value="Printer queue %1 already exists on the destination computer. Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) will update the printer settings to match the settings on the source computer. No user action is required." />
        <string id="event_MSG_QUEUE_KEEPEXISTING" value="Printer queue %1 already exists on the destination computer and will not be changed because Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) was run in 'Keep Existing' mode. No user action is required." />
        <string id="event_MSG_QUEUE_PORTFAIL" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) failed to restore print queue %1 because port %2 is unknown. Printbrm.exe will attempt to restore the print queue on port FILE: instead. This can occur if the backup file contains incomplete data about the port, or if the port or port settings are incompatible with the version of Windows installed on the destination computer. Recreate the affected port on the destination computer and then change the print queue to use the new port." />
        <string id="event_MSG_QUEUE_PORTFILE" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) successfully installed printer queue %1 on port FILE:. No user action is required." />
        <string id="event_MSG_QUEUE_PORTFILEFAIL" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) failed to restore printer queue %1 on port FILE:. Error %2. This can occur if the backup file contains incomplete data about the port, or if the port or port settings are incompatible with the version of Windows installed on the destination computer. Recreate the affected port on the destination computer." />
        <string id="event_MSG_QUEUE_BACKUP_FAIL" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) failed to back up print queue %1. The backup process will continue, skipping this queue. Error: %2." />
        <string id="event_MSG_QUEUE_RESTORE_FAIL" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) failed to restore print queue %1. The restore process will continue, skipping this queue. Error: %2." />
        <string id="event_MSG_PPROC_SUCCESS" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) successfully restored %1. No user action is required." />
        <string id="event_MSG_PPROC_FAIL" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) failed to restore print processor %1 while restoring print queues from a file.  Error: %2. Examine the Windows error returned by this event to determine the cause." />
        <string id="event_MSG_PPROC_BACKUP_FAIL" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) failed to back up print processor %1 (%2). Error: %3." />
        <string id="event_MSG_PPROC_BACKUP_FILES_FAIL" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) failed to back up print processor files for architecture %1. Error: %2." />
        <string id="event_MSG_LMON_BACKUP_DEPFAIL" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) could not back up a dependent file for a language monitor. Dependent file: %1. Error: %2. This can occur if the file was deleted or moved." />
        <string id="event_MSG_LMON_SUCCESS" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) successfully restored language monitor %1. No user action is required." />
        <string id="event_MSG_LMON_FAIL" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) could not restore language monitor %1 while restoring print queues from a file. Check the print queue backup file and examine the Windows error returned by this event to determine the cause." />
        <string id="event_MSG_LMON_ARCH" value="The language monitors in the backup file are for a different processor architecture than the destination computer. Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) will not migrate any language monitors. Source architecture: %1. Destination architecture: %2." />
        <string id="event_MSG_DRV_DIFFARCH_WARN" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) restored a driver for a different processor architecture than that of the destination computer. Printbrm.exe will attempt to locate and install a native version of driver %1 on destination %2. Try to print to the print queue, and if necessary, manually install a native version of the driver." />
        <string id="event_MSG_DRV_DIFFARCH_SUCCESS" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) successfully installed driver %1 for the processor architecture of the destination computer. No user action is required." />
        <string id="event_MSG_DRV_DIFFARCH_FAIL" value="The driver in the backup file is for a different processor architecture than the destination computer, and Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) could not locate and install a native version of the driver. Driver: %1. Destination architecture: %2. Error: %3. Install a native version of the driver on the destination computer and then retry importing the print queues." />
        <string id="event_MSG_DRV_CAB_SUCCESS" value="Printbrm.exe (the Printer Migration Wizard or the command-line tool) successfully installed driver %1 (%2) from a cabinet (.cab) file. No user action is re
